zh.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau於1135年出生在比利時海瑙特。他和Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches至少有2個女兒。他去世於1219年11月8日之後。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: l'uncle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Old Scandinavian personal name Úlfketel, Úlfkell. Compare Anketell . Some of the later bearers may belong with sense (2).
2 English: relationship name from Middle English uncle, Old French oncle ‘uncle’. Compare Eames and Neame .

variants.name: Eames
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

variants.name: Uncles
meanings: English: variant of Uncle with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Anketell
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from the Anglo-Norman French personal name An(s)ketill, a Norman form of Old Scandinavian Ásketill influenced by Continental Germanic names in Ans-. The Scandinavian name is from ás ‘(pagan) god’ + ketill ‘kettle, cauldron’. Compare Antell , Askell , Axtell , Ashkettle , and Uncle , all from other variants of the same personal name.

en.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau was born in 1135, in Hainaut, Belgium. He had at least 2 daughters with Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. He died after 8 November 1219.

fr.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau est né en 1135, à Hainaut, Belgique. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. Il est décédé après 8 novembre 1219.

de.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau wurde 1135 in Hennegau, Belgien geboren. Er hatte mit Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb nach 8. November 1219.

it.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau è nato il nel 1135 nel Hainaut, Belgio. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. Morì dopo il 8 novembre 1219.

ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateauは1135年にベルギーヘイナトで生まれた。彼はMahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Rachesとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1219年11月8日以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau(은)는 1135년 벨기에 하인오트에서 태어났다. 그는 Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1219년 11월 8일 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau nasceu em 1135, em Hainaut, Bélgica. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. Ele faleceu após 8 de novembro de 1219.

ru.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau родился в 1135 г. в местности ханаут, Бельгия. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. Он умер после 8 ноябрь 1219 г.

es.lifeSummary: Guillaume l'uncle de Hainaut, Seigneur of Werchin et Thy le Chateau nació en 1135, en Hainaut, Bélgica. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Mahaut de Lalaing, Dame de Raches. Murió después de 8 de noviembre de 1219.
